name: Lu Shu
page_title: Lu Shu 魯淑 [Wu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
ranks: General Who is Illustrious and Martial
historical_notes:
Born after his death, Lu Su had a son who was very strong who was praised by the supervisor of Ruxu, Zhang Cheng. In the middle of [the reign of] Yong An [AD 258–264], he was entitled the General Who is Illustrious and Martial (Zhao Wu Jiang Jun) and Marquis of the Chief Village (Dou Ting Hou), and supervisor of Wuchang. In the middle of [the reign of] Jian Heng [AD 269–271], he was promoted to supervise Xiakou where his administration was strict and orderly and this showed how capable he was. In the third year of Feng Huang [AD 274], he died (1)

1. From Lu Su’s SGZ

full_text:
Lu Shu 魯淑
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Lu Shu (魯淑). The son of Lu Su. Praised as a capable military commander. Lu Shu was affiliated with the Wu Kingdom. Lu Shu lived during the Three Kingdoms period of Chinese history.

BiographyHistoric (Confirmed)
Born after his death, Lu Su had a son who was very strong who was praised by the supervisor of Ruxu, Zhang Cheng. In the middle of [the reign of] Yong An [AD 258–264], he was entitled the General Who is Illustrious and Martial (Zhao Wu Jiang Jun) and Marquis of the Chief Village (Dou Ting Hou), and supervisor of Wuchang. In the middle of [the reign of] Jian Heng [AD 269–271], he was promoted to supervise Xiakou where his administration was strict and orderly and this showed how capable he was. In the third year of Feng Huang [AD 274], he died (1)
